MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-23-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
ORDER OVERRULING IN PART AND SUSTAINING IN PART DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS LESTER SCHWARTZ, LINDA SCHWARTZ, BLUE STONE INVESTMENTS, LLC AND VELCO INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. MATTER ARGUED AND SUBMITTED BY COUNSEL. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: OVERRULED IN PART, AND SUSTAINED IN PART. DEFENDANTS BASE THEIR DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION ON THE FACT THAT THE RENTAL AGREEMENT ATTACHED TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT CONTAINED A RELEASE PROVISION. PLAINTIFF, IN RESPONSE TO DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER, RAISES A NUMBER OF FACTUAL ISSUES REGARDING THE ENFORCEABILITY OF THE EXCULPATORY CLAUSE. SPECIFICALLY, PLAINTIFF CONTENDS THAT THE PROVISION IS UNENFORCEABLE BECAUSE IT WAS UNCLEAR, AMBIGUOUS, NOT EXPLICIT, AND A VIOLATION OF PUBLIC POLICY. (PLAINTIFF'S EX. A; SEE LEON V. FAMILY FITNESS CENTER (NO. 107) INC. (1998) 61 CAL.APP.4TH 1227; SEE TUNKL V. REGENTS OF UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA (1963) 60 CAL.2D 92.) THE DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED FACTUAL ISSUES REGARDING THE ENFORCEABILITY OF THE RELEASE PROVISION THAT ARE NOT SUITED FOR DETERMINATION ON A DEMURRER. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS ALSO OVERRULED BECAUSE IT IS BASED ON A PREMISES LIABILITY THEORY, WHEREAS THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION IS BASED ON AN ACTIVE NEGLIGENCE THEORY. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE IT IS NOT DISTINCT FROM THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY. THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND BECAUSE IT DOES NOT INVOLVE AN INDEPENDENT RIGHT. THE ISSUE OF WHETHER DEFENDANTS OWED A DUTY TO PLAINTIFF IS FULLY ADDRESSED IN THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Set For Trial On Sep-26-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Set For Trial On Aug-15-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Feb-10-2016 Off Calendar

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-23-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. NATHANIEL M. LEEDS, ESQ. of Callaway & Wolf, Tel No. 415-541-0300 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. LOREN E. SCHWARTZ, ESQ. of Dunn & Panagotacos LLP, Tel No. 415-982-2100 appearing for Defendants Lester Schwartz, Linda Schwartz, Blue Stone Investments, LLC, and Velco Incorporated.

RULING - Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS LESTER SCHWARTZ, LINDA SCHWARTZ, BLUE STONE INVESTMENTS, LLC AND VELCO INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled in part, and sustained in part. Defendants base their demurrer to the first and second causes of action on the fact that the rental agreement attached to Plaintiff's complaint contained a release provision. Plaintiff, in response to Defendants' demurrer, raises a number of factual issues regarding the enforceability of the exculpatory clause. Specifically, Plaintiff contends that the provision is unenforceable because it was unclear, ambiguous, not explicit, and a violation of public policy. (Plaintiff's Ex. A; See Leon v. Family Fitness Center (No. 107) Inc. (1998) 61 Cal.App.4th 1227; See Tunkl v. Regents of University of California (1963) 60 Cal.2d 92.) The demurrer to the first and second causes of action is overruled because Plaintiff has raised factual issues regarding the enforceability of the release provision that are not suited for determination on a demurrer. Defendants' demurrer to the second cause of action is also overruled because it is based on a premises liability theory, whereas the first cause of action is based on an active negligence theory. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained without leave to amend because it is not distinct from the second cause of action for premises liability. The fourth cause of action for declaratory relief is sustained without leave to amend because it does not involve an independent right. The issue of whether Defendants owed a duty to Plaintiff is fully addressed in the first and second causes of action. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-20-2015 Continued To Feb-10-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610
